name: Ministry of Mining and Critical Minerals

goals.short_name: British Columbians can access and maintain mineral, placer and coal rights

main_services.name: Acquire and maintain a mineral, placer or coal title
Search title records and explore recreationally
Meet mineral and coal tax obligations

goals.short_name: Mines are permitted, operated and closed safely and responsibly

main_services.name: Get a mine permitted from exploration through closure
Meet mine health, safety and certification requirements

goals.short_name: B.C.'s critical minerals economy and geoscience knowledge base grow

acts.summary: The primary statute authorizing the registration of mineral and placer titles with the province and providing the policy framework for mineral titles administration, including free miner certificates, claims and leases.

sections.title: Free miner certificate and right of entry
sections.description: Only a free miner, or an agent of a free miner, may enter mineral lands to explore for minerals or placer minerals.

services.name: Get a Free Miner Certificate
services.ms_name: Acquire and maintain a mineral, placer or coal title

services.name: Pan for gold at a recreational hand panning reserve
services.ms_name: Search title records and explore recreationally

sections.title: Mineral and placer claims
sections.description: Sets out how mineral and placer claims are located, recorded and maintained through Mineral Titles Online.

services.name: Register a mineral or placer claim online
services.ms_name: Acquire and maintain a mineral, placer or coal title

sections.title: Mineral and placer leases
sections.description: Governs conversion of a recorded claim into a mineral or placer lease.

services.name: Apply for a mineral or placer lease
services.ms_name: Acquire and maintain a mineral, placer or coal title

acts.summary: Regulates all mining activities in British Columbia during exploration, development, construction, production, closure, reclamation and abandonment, including permits, reclamation security, certification and enforcement.

sections.title: Permit to work in, on or about a mine
sections.description: Before starting any work in, on or about a mine, the owner, agent or manager must hold a permit issued by the chief permitting officer.

services.name: Apply for a Mines Act permit to explore for minerals or coal
services.ms_name: Get a mine permitted from exploration through closure

services.name: Apply for a Mines Act permit to construct or operate a mine
services.ms_name: Get a mine permitted from exploration through closure

sections.title: Permit security for reclamation
sections.description: The chief permitting officer may require security as a condition of a permit to provide for protection of, and mitigation of damage to, land and watercourses affected by the mine.

services.name: Post a reclamation security for a mine
services.ms_name: Get a mine permitted from exploration through closure

sections.title: Reclamation compliance orders
sections.description: If an owner, agent, manager or permittee fails to comply with reclamation requirements in the code, regulations or permit, the chief inspector may issue an order to remedy the failure.

services.name: Submit a mine reclamation and closure plan
services.ms_name: Get a mine permitted from exploration through closure

sections.title: Certification of persons employed at a mine
sections.description: Each manager must ensure every person employed at a mine, if required by the regulations or code, is under the daily supervision of a person who holds a valid and appropriate certificate.

services.name: Get a mine supervisor, shiftboss or blaster certification
services.ms_name: Meet mine health, safety and certification requirements

sections.title: Investigation of incidents
sections.description: On the direction of the chief inspector, an inspector must investigate and report on an incident that has caused, or had the potential to cause, serious personal injury, loss of life or property, or environmental damage.

services.name: Report a mine safety incident or hazard
services.ms_name: Meet mine health, safety and certification requirements

sections.title: Health, Safety and Reclamation Code
sections.description: The minister must establish a health, safety and reclamation code committee to prepare and maintain a code dealing with all aspects of health, safety and reclamation in the operation of a mine.

services.name: Follow the Health, Safety and Reclamation Code for Mines
services.ms_name: Meet mine health, safety and certification requirements

acts.summary: Authorizes the registration of coal titles with the province and provides the policy framework for coal licence and lease administration.

sections.title: Application for a coal licence
sections.description: A person may apply to the minister for a coal licence in the form and manner the minister specifies.

services.name: Apply for a coal licence or lease
services.ms_name: Acquire and maintain a mineral, placer or coal title

sections.title: Application for a coal lease
sections.description: A licensee or lessee may apply for a coal lease, converting exploration rights into production rights over the location.

services.name: Apply for a coal licence or lease
services.ms_name: Acquire and maintain a mineral, placer or coal title

acts.summary: Imposes mineral tax on the commercial production of coal and mineral resources from B.C. mines, quarries and placer operations to compensate the province for the depletion of those resources.

sections.title: Self-assessment and payment of mineral tax
sections.description: Mine operators must self-assess and pay mineral tax on taxable resources removed from their operations unless an exemption applies.

services.name: Pay mineral tax on mine production
services.ms_name: Meet mineral and coal tax obligations

acts.summary: Imposes an annual tax on owners of freehold mineral rights, with the rate based on the size of the mineral land and whether minerals are being produced from it.

sections.title: Annual tax on mineral land
sections.description: Not later than July 2 in every year, an owner of mineral land must pay to the minister a tax at a rate based on the size of the owner's total mineral land.

services.name: Pay mineral land tax on freehold mineral rights
services.ms_name: Meet mineral and coal tax obligations
